pins.title: Reynisfjara
pins.text: Hazardous conditions caused by stormy weather and high waves. Although sneaker waves are not currently at red alert level, the beach is best viewed from the viewpoint. The beach itself is not accessible close to the viewpoint. Please respect the closures and safety instructions.

pins.title: Surge in Dyngjujökull
pins.text: Surge has started in Dyngjujökull. People are advised not to travel on Dyngjujökull. Crevasses have probably already started to form in areas on the glacier which in the past 20 years has been uncrevassed and safe to travel across.

pins.title: Glymur
pins.text: The log (bridge) is not in place during winter.

pins.title: Kverkfjöll
pins.text: Geothermal activity in the area has increased a lot. Due to this, the trail up to the hut (Jöklarannsóknarfélag Íslands) has gotten a lot thinner and many crevasses have formed. Closed for driving/hiking/skiing.

pins.title: Rauðfeldsgjá
pins.text: Inside the canyon, rocks and ice have fallen from above, causing accidents and injury. Only go inside in summer when weather is dry and in winter when temperature is lower than -5°C.

pins.title: Grindavík area
pins.text: Grindavik is open. Crevasses can be expected so stay on paths. The area is still considered hazardous due to volcanic unrest. In case of elevated threat level, evacuation sirens will be activated. Take notice where exits are to get out of town.

pins.title: Stóra-Sandvík beach
pins.text: Very strong rip currents! Surfers be extra careful.

pins.title: Between Hof and Fjallsárlón
pins.text: Road no. 1 between Skaftafell and Fjallsárlón/Jökulsárlón: To keep in mind when it's windy, there are often extreme wind gusts on that part.

pins.title: Hjörleifshöfði
pins.text: River North of Hjörleifshöfði is only passable with bigger 4x4, not for Dacia Duster and similar vehicles

pins.title: Krýsuvíkurberg
pins.text: Big fissures in the sea cliffs and danger of landslide. Respect fences and warnings.

pins.title: Jökulsárlón / Fjallsárlón
pins.text: Do not walk on ice or icebergs! There is danger of falling into the ice cold water which can be fatal.

pins.title: Keilir mountain
pins.text: Road to Keilir is only for bigger 4x4. No small cars.

pins.title: Reynisfjara
pins.text: Hazardous conditions at the beach caused by stormy weather and high waves. Even though sneaker waves are not at red alert level, the beach is only accessible from the viewpoint. Please respect the closures and safety instructions.

pins.title: Valahnúkur
pins.text: CLOSED due to dangerous condition! New cracks have formed due to earthquackes in 2025. Valahnúkur is a tuff mountain that is slowly eroding. Risk of landslides. Do not hike up or stay close to it's steep slopes.

pins.title: Reykjadalur
pins.text: Stay on paths and do not walk elsewhere in this area. Boiling springs may be hidden, easy to step through and get burned. This applies in all geothermal areas in Iceland.

pins.title: Geysir
pins.text: Stay on paths and do not walk elsewhere in this area. Boiling springs may be hidden, easy to step through and get burned. Paths can be slippery, use crampons. This applies in all geothermal areas in Iceland.

pins.title: Gunnuhver
pins.text: The whole area around Gunnuhver is high geothermal and dangerous boiling hot springs in many places - even hidden, easy to step through and get badly burned. Stay on paths and do not walk elsewhere!

pins.title: Bridge between continents
pins.text: Large crevasses have opened up in the area. Stay on marked paths.

pins.title: Brúarfoss waterfall
pins.text: Use the main trail. Stay away from the river itself and the river banks, they can be insecure and very slippery. A fall into the river can be fatal.

pins.title: F-225 (Dómadalsleið)
pins.text: Large crack in the road. The road is marked closed. Do not drive the road.

pins.title: Kerið
pins.text: Do NOT walk on the ice. The ice is not safe and falling through the ice is extremely dangerous and even fatal.
